economic_notes: First Nation has investments in multiple limited partnerships including Sema:th Xo:tsa Developments LP (land development), Sth'oqi Aquaculture LP, Kilgard Business Park LP, and Wochmel Security LP. Trans Mountain Pipeline relationship agreement generated $2,703,195 in funding. Settlement Trust (Upper Sumas IR6 Right-of-Way) holds restricted assets. First Nation receives 45% of revenue from ISC (down from 57% prior year). Guarantees placed on member loans up to $4,000,000 facility; $3,118,417 drawn. Also guarantees loans of LPs/Corporations totalling $9,608,946.
notes: Fiscal year ended March 31, 2022. Four subsidiaries consolidated using modified equity method: Sumas Clay Products Ltd. (100%), SFN Renewable Energy Ltd. (100%), Sema:th Xot:sa Developments Ltd. (100%), and 1099212 B.C. Ltd. (50%). Three GBEs accounted for by modified equity method: SXDLP, SALP, KBPLP (plus WSLP). Partnerships have December 31 year-ends creating a 3-month lag. Settlement Trust (Upper Sumas IR6) consolidated as at December 31, 2021; trust funds held for minors ($601,405) excluded from financial statements. Loss on partnerships of $1,011,102 is a significant non-cash item. Total government transfers estimated as ISC ($3,984,158) + ISC contracts ($4,483,962) + CMHC/housing ($990,669) + First Nations Health Authority ($579,595) + Province of BC ($368,904) + Sto:lo Service Agency ($267,141) + Dept of Natural Resources ($234,398) + FNESC ($148,621) + New Relationship Trust ($20,000) + FNLMRC ($12,500) + Trans Mountain ($2,703,195) minus items not qualifying as government transfers. No schedule of remuneration included in document. Deferred revenue change shown in cash flow as $7,269,449 increase but opening/closing balances not separately disclosed on face of balance sheet. CMHC housing mortgage of $949,297 received during year. 12 housing units referenced (six units per two CMHC mortgages).
